text:
Operation Streamline is a zero-tolerance immigration enforcement program created by Congress in 2005 that mandates the federal criminal prosecution of all illegal aliens caught crossing the border. Based on ethnographic research in the form of multiple court visits and interviews with judges and law enforcement officials at all four border judicial districts, this article investigates the legitimacy, effects, and the operation of the enforcement program. Our findings are that the enforcement program has saturated the federal courts along the U.S.-Mexico border with charges against thousands of low-skilled, economic migrants, who would not previously have been charged with a crime, and has therefore diverted the attention of federal criminal justice resources from more serious crimes—such as murder, assault, narcotics trafficking and sex trafficking—previously investigated and prosecuted by the Border Patrol.

What follows is a more detailed summary of our analysis. Our analysis also addresses the deterrent effect of the program as well as several constitutional issues raised by its operation. We have found that the program is unlikely to act as a deterrent to unlawful entry into the United States. While the border apprehensions have declined in each of the three years in which the Program has been in effect, the decline in apprehensions has almost precisely tracked the decline in the U.S. economy. Furthermore, the assembly-line justice system of holding and promptly trying en masse aliens who were apprehended while attempting to cross the border without documentation violates core provisions of the U.S. Constitution as well as the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure. We conclude with the recommendation that the administration end Operation Streamline, restore prosecutorial discretion to U.S. Attorneys who must make the charging decisions for alien crimes in the federal courts, and return first-time crossers of the border to the civil system where they can be detained and deported in a manner that is fully compliant with the Constitution and that protects the national security of the United States.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
